title: erlang BIFs batch 3 — system, memory, and port stubs
purpose: OTP modules query system state via erlang:statistics/1, erlang:memory/0, erlang:ports/0, and various system introspection BIFs. Most can be stubbed with reasonable defaults — beamr doesn't have ports or full statistics, but returning empty lists or zero values prevents import failures.
task: Register system introspection BIF stubs that return sensible defaults.

requirements.title: erlang:statistics/1 stubs
requirements.spec: Return basic statistics for commonly queried items.

requirements.title: erlang:memory/0 and erlang:memory/1
acceptance: memory() returns proplist with total, processes, system, atom, binary keys
memory(total) returns total memory in bytes
Values are approximate (sum of heap sizes + atom table + binary heap)

requirements.title: Port stubs
requirements.spec: Stub port-related BIFs that OTP queries during startup.
acceptance: These stubs prevent import failures without implementing the port subsystem

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement the port subsystem — stubs only
SHALL NOT implement full statistics tracking — approximate values from available data
SHALL return sensible defaults that don't crash OTP code
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
